confirmations.reason: 'Strict Mode' restricts the agent's access to external resources and sensitive operations as a general security lockdown, but it does not specifically defend against malicious instructions injected via tool outputs or web content. Access restriction and prompt-injection defense are distinct mechanisms; the former is access surface control, the latter is input sanitization/detection.

A 'Fine-Grained Permissions Engine' governs what the agent is allowed to do by gating operations behind permission checks. This is authorization/access control, not scanning of code or agent actions against a threat model for security vulnerabilities. The two are category-adjacent but distinct.

Building a Docker image from source is part of a containerization workflow, but 'dev-container integration' specifically means running the agent inside a pre-defined dev container spec (e.g. devcontainer.json) as an isolated runtime environment. Image-build capability does not confirm the agent runs inside a defined dev container for its own execution context.

'Artifact Review Policy' controls how a user reviews and approves agent-proposed plans and artifacts before changes are committed — this is a human approval gate, not the agent autonomously reviewing diffs/PRs for issues. Automated code review requires the agent to analyze and report on code quality/issues, which is the inverse of what is described.

'Verification loops' describe the agent running local test commands (unit tests, build scripts) as part of its own iterative coding workflow to validate its own changes. This is self-verification during code generation, not a harness for running structured evaluations against the agent's behavior as a subject under test.

'Session management' explicitly supports rewinding and forking conversation sessions, which directly maps to the checkpoint/rewind capability of saving state and rolling back to prior points. This is a direct match.

'Scheduled Tasks' allows users to schedule messages to agents on a time-based trigger. This is asynchronous task scheduling, not remote control/steering of an active agent run from a mobile or remote client. The capability requires interactive remote oversight; scheduling is a different interaction model.

'Scheduled Tasks' sends scheduled messages to agents, not push notifications to users about run progress or completion. The direction of communication is inverted: notifications push status to the user, whereas scheduled tasks push inputs to the agent.
